market_data.role: Current normalized Polymarket orderbook snapshot fetch path
market_data.notes: Uses Polymarket/Gamma/CLOB-style REST orderbook access, not websocket streaming.

market_data.role: Market discovery / metadata path for tradable Polymarket markets
MLB mapping layer between discovered markets and baseball-specific handling

user_order_fill_data.notes: Tracks paper opens/closes, not realtime Polymarket user stream events.
No Polymarket user websocket/fill subscription layer present.

existing_reusable_websocket_client_layer.details: No existing reusable websocket/client layer for Polymarket market stream or user/order/fill stream was found in the allowed discovery set. Existing integration is REST/poll based.

dashboard_server_hosting_assessment.details: dashboard_server.py already hosts the SSE fanout to the browser and can consume a server-side local state source. But a clean server-side market/user stream source is still missing and should not be crammed directly into dashboard_server.py if realtime Polymarket streams are added.

recommended_first_realtime_slice.scope: Server-side Polymarket market stream only for tracked open-position assets
recommended_first_realtime_slice.why_first: It directly replaces the current held-side current-price motion path with true market-realtime inputs while keeping user/fill stream work separate for the next step.
recommended_first_realtime_slice.fallback: Keep current REST/poll snapshot path and current browser polling intact when stream is absent or stale.
recommended_first_realtime_slice.odds_api_rule: Odds API remains verification-only and non-streaming.

conclusion: Reusable REST-based Polymarket market data integration already exists, but no reusable websocket/user-stream layer was found. New narrow stream/state files are justified for implementation.
